learningObjectives: Name common food and drink items using Starters vocabulary.
Use like, eat and drink in present simple sentences.
Say what you do not like using don't like.
Read short sentences about food and choose matching pictures.
Listen and identify food and drink items in short dialogues.

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: I eat an egg for breakfast.
We eat cake on my birthday.
I am hungry. I want rice.

grammarReference.explanation: Use like for food you enjoy. Use eat for food and drink for liquids. Add -s with he, she, it: He likes rice.
commonMistakes: He like rice (×) → He likes rice (✓)
I drink chicken (×) → I eat chicken (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use don't like to say you do not enjoy something. Don't = do not.
commonMistakes: I not like fish (×) → I don't like fish (✓)
I don't likes rice (×) → I don't like rice (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Food and Drink Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand fourteen food and drink words at Starters level.

exercises.title: Learn: Food Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: A yellow fruit that monkeys like is a _____.
questions.explanation: Banana is a yellow fruit. Orange is also a fruit but it is orange, not yellow.

choices.distractorNote: Fruit but orange colour, not yellow

questions.questionText: White drink from cows is _____.
questions.explanation: Milk is a white drink. Water is clear; juice is from fruit.

choices.distractorNote: Clear drink, not white from cows

questions.questionText: Birds lay this. We fry it for breakfast.
questions.explanation: An egg comes from birds. Chicken is meat; rice is a grain.

choices.distractorNote: Meat from birds, not what birds lay

questions.questionText: A red or green round fruit is an _____.
questions.explanation: An apple is a round fruit. Cake is sweet food; fish lives in water.

choices.distractorNote: Sweet baked food, not a fruit
Animal in water, not a fruit

questions.questionText: We eat this with meals in Vietnam. It is white.
questions.explanation: Rice is a common white food. Bread is also eaten but is not always white grains.

choices.distractorNote: Drink, not food you eat with a spoon

questions.questionText: When you want food, you feel _____.
questions.explanation: Hungry means you want to eat. Lunch is a meal time, not a feeling.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the food and drink words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Each word matches one clear meaning. Learners often confuse drink words milk and water.

pairs.right: clear drink you need every day

exercises.title: Check: Food and Drink Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: A yellow fruit that monkeys like is a _____.
questions.explanation: Banana (chuối) — trái cây màu vàng.

questions.questionText: White drink from cows is _____.
questions.explanation: Milk (sữa) — đồ uống màu trắng.

questions.questionText: Complete with food or drink words.
questions.explanation: rice = cơm; water = nước uống.

content.template: I eat [0] for lunch. I drink [1].

exercises.title: Review: Food and Drink Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: A yellow fruit that monkeys like is a _____.
questions.explanation: Banana (chuối) — trái cây màu vàng.

questions.questionText: White drink from cows is _____.
questions.explanation: Milk (sữa) — đồ uống màu trắng.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Fruit and Drinks
lessons.learningObjective: Name fruit and drinks: apple, banana, orange, milk, water and juice.

questions.questionText: A round red or green fruit is an _____.
questions.explanation: Apple (táo) — trái cây tròn.

questions.questionText: Orange _____ comes from oranges.
questions.explanation: Orange juice — nước ép cam.

questions.questionText: We drink _____ when we are thirsty.
questions.explanation: Water (nước) — uống khi khát.

questions.questionText: Match each word to its type.
questions.explanation: Phân biệt trái cây và đồ uống.

questions.questionText: Which is a drink, not food?
questions.explanation: Water — uống, không ăn.

questions.questionText: Mother buys orange _____.
questions.explanation: Orange juice — trong bài mua sắm.
Điền trái cây hoặc đồ uống.

questions.questionText: Which is a drink, not food?
questions.explanation: Water — uống, không ăn.

questions.questionText: Mother buys orange _____.
questions.explanation: Orange juice — trong bài mua sắm.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Meals and Main Food
lessons.learningObjective: Use egg, rice, chicken, bread, fish and hungry in context.

questions.questionText: We eat _____ for lunch at school.
questions.explanation: Rice (cơm) — bữa trưa.

questions.questionText: When your stomach is empty, you are _____.
questions.explanation: Hungry (đói) — bụng trống.

questions.questionText: _____ comes from chickens — we eat it.
questions.explanation: Chicken (thịt gà) — đồ ăn.
Ghép từ đồ ăn với bữa ăn.

questions.questionText: We don't eat _____ for breakfast in the text.
questions.explanation: We don't eat chicken for breakfast.

questions.questionText: Anna eats an _____ at lunch.
questions.explanation: She eats an apple.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence about being hungry.
questions.explanation: I am + hungry.

questions.questionText: We don't eat _____ for breakfast in the text.
questions.explanation: We don't eat chicken for breakfast.

questions.questionText: Anna eats an _____ at lunch.
questions.explanation: She eats an apple.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Like, Eat and Drink
lessons.learningObjective: Use like, eat, drink and don't like in sentences about food.

exercises.title: Learn: Like, Eat or Drink
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence.

questions.questionText: I _____ apples. They are yummy!
questions.explanation: Use like to say you enjoy food. Eat means you put food in your mouth.

choices.distractorNote: Possible but like fits enjoyment better here
Apples are food, not a drink

questions.questionText: She _____ milk every morning.
questions.explanation: Use drinks with she (third person -s). Milk is a liquid.

choices.distractorNote: Grammatically possible but drinks fits the action

questions.questionText: I _____ fish. It smells bad to me.
questions.explanation: Don't like shows you do not enjoy something. The clue 'smells bad' tells us it is negative.

questions.questionText: We _____ rice and chicken for lunch.
questions.explanation: Use eat for solid food like rice and chicken.

choices.distractorNote: Rice and chicken are not drinks

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences with like, likes, eat, eats, drink or drinks.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] bananas. | He [1] water. | They [2] chicken.
questions.explanation: I like (no -s); He drinks (third person -s, liquid); They eat (plural, solid food).

content.template: I [0] bananas. He [1] water. They [2] chicken.

exercises.title: Check: Like, Eat and Drink Quiz

questions.questionText: I _____ apples. They are yummy!
questions.explanation: I like — chủ ngữ I không thêm -s.

questions.questionText: Anna _____ like fish.
questions.explanation: Anna doesn't like — ngôi thứ ba số ít.
like/eat cho đồ ăn; drink cho đồ uống.

exercises.title: Review: Like, Eat and Drink Quiz

questions.questionText: I _____ apples. They are yummy!
questions.explanation: I like — chủ ngữ I không thêm -s.

questions.questionText: Anna _____ like fish.
questions.explanation: Anna doesn't like — ngôi thứ ba số ít.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Like, Eat and Drink
lessons.learningObjective: Use like, eat and drink correctly with food and drink nouns.

questions.questionText: I _____ rice for lunch.
questions.explanation: Eat — ăn đồ rắn như cơm.

questions.questionText: She _____ milk every day.
questions.explanation: She drinks — ngôi thứ ba thêm -s.
like = thích; eat = ăn; drink = uống.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Tom likes — he/she + -s.

choices.distractorNote: Banana số ít — Tom thích nhiều

questions.questionText: You _____ chicken. It is food.
questions.explanation: Eat chicken — ăn thức ăn.
I like + đồ ăn.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Tom likes — he/she + -s.

choices.distractorNote: Banana số ít — Tom thích nhiều

questions.questionText: You _____ chicken. It is food.
questions.explanation: Eat chicken — ăn thức ăn.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: I Don't Like
lessons.learningObjective: Say what you do not like using don't like and doesn't like.

questions.questionText: I _____ like fish.
questions.explanation: I don't like — phủ định với I.

questions.questionText: She _____ like eggs.
questions.explanation: She doesn't like — ngôi thứ ba.
don't với I/you/we/they; doesn't với he/she/it.

content.template: I [0] like fish. Anna [1] like fish.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: I don't like — chuẩn.

questions.questionText: Tom _____ like eggs.
questions.explanation: Tom doesn't — he + doesn't.

questions.questionText: We _____ like fish for breakfast.
questions.explanation: We don't like — we + don't.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: I don't like — chuẩn.

questions.questionText: Tom _____ like eggs.
questions.explanation: Tom doesn't — he + doesn't.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Lunch Time
lessons.learningObjective: Read short sentences about food and answer questions about main idea, details and vocabulary.

exercises.title: Learn: Read: Tom's Lunch
exercises.instructions: Read the text. Then answer the questions.

passage.title: Tom's Lunch
passage.text:
Tom is hungry. It is lunch time.

Tom likes rice. He eats rice every day. He likes chicken too. He drinks water with his lunch.

Tom does not like fish. He does not like eggs.

His friend Anna likes apples and juice. She eats an apple after lunch. Tom likes cake on his birthday.
passage.imagePrompt: A boy at a table with rice, chicken, water and an apple; a girl with juice nearby.

questions.questionText: What is the main idea of the text?
questions.explanation: The text is about what Tom and Anna like and eat for lunch. It is not about school or toys.

choices.text: Tom and Anna talk about food they like.

questions.questionText: What does Tom drink with lunch?
questions.explanation: The text says: He drinks water with his lunch.

choices.distractorNote: Common drink but not in text
Anna drinks juice, not Tom at lunch

questions.questionText: Why does Tom not eat fish?
questions.explanation: The text says Tom does not like fish — so he does not enjoy it.

questions.questionText: In 'She eats an apple after lunch', what does an apple mean?
questions.explanation: An apple is a fruit. Anna eats it as food after lunch.

questions.questionText: Put the ideas in order: (1) Tom is hungry (2) Tom says what he likes and does not like (3) Anna's food
questions.explanation: The text starts with Tom hungry, then his likes/dislikes, then Anna.

passage.title: Lunch at School
passage.text:
It is twelve o'clock. We eat lunch at school.

I have rice and chicken. My friend eats an egg and bread.

She drinks milk. I drink water. Tom likes bananas.

Anna doesn't like fish. She eats an apple. We are not hungry now!
passage.imagePrompt: School lunch trays with rice, chicken, egg, fruit and drinks.

questions.questionText: What does the writer eat?
questions.explanation: I have rice and chicken.

questions.questionText: What does the friend drink?
questions.explanation: She drinks milk.

passage.title: Lunch at School
passage.text:

She drinks milk. I drink water. Tom likes bananas.

Anna doesn't like fish. She eats an apple. We are not hungry now!
passage.imagePrompt: School lunch trays with rice, chicken, egg, fruit and drinks.

questions.questionText: What time is lunch?
questions.explanation: It is twelve o'clock.

questions.questionText: What doesn't Anna like?
questions.explanation: Anna doesn't like fish.

questions.questionText: How do they feel after lunch?
questions.explanation: We are not hungry now!

passage.title: Lunch at School
passage.text:

passage.imagePrompt: School lunch trays with rice, chicken, egg, fruit and drinks.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Shopping for Food
lessons.learningObjective: Read for fruit, drinks and shopping details in a short text.

passage.title: Shopping for Food
passage.text:
Mother and I go to the shop. We buy apples, oranges and bananas.

"Do you like juice?" asks Mother. "Yes! Orange juice!" I say.

We get milk and water too. Father likes chicken for dinner.

We also buy a small cake. Yummy!
passage.imagePrompt: Grocery shop with fruit, milk, juice and cake on shelves.

questions.questionText: What fruit do they buy?
questions.explanation: We buy apples, oranges and bananas.

questions.questionText: What juice does the child want?
questions.explanation: Yes! Orange juice!

passage.title: Shopping for Food

"Do you like juice?" asks Mother. "Yes! Orange juice!" I say.

We get milk and water too. Father likes chicken for dinner.

questions.explanation: Father likes chicken for dinner.

choices.distractorNote: Bánh — mua nhưng không phải bữa tối

questions.questionText: What sweet food do they buy?
questions.explanation: We also buy a small cake.

questions.explanation: We get milk and water too.

questions.explanation: Bài về mua thực phẩm.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Breakfast Time
lessons.learningObjective: Understand breakfast foods and don't like in a family text.

passage.title: Breakfast Time
passage.text:
Good morning! I am hungry. For breakfast I eat an egg and bread.

My sister drinks milk. My brother eats a banana.

Mother says: "Eat your apple!" Father drinks water.

We don't eat chicken for breakfast. We eat fruit and drink milk.
passage.imagePrompt: Family breakfast table with egg, bread, fruit and milk.

questions.questionText: What does the writer eat for breakfast?
questions.explanation: I eat an egg and bread.

questions.questionText: What does the sister drink?
questions.explanation: My sister drinks milk.

passage.title: Breakfast Time
passage.text:
Good morning! I am hungry. For breakfast I eat an egg and bread.

My sister drinks milk. My brother eats a banana.

Mother says: "Eat your apple!" Father drinks water.

We don't eat chicken for breakfast. We eat fruit and drink milk.
passage.imagePrompt: Family breakfast table with egg, bread, fruit and milk.

questions.questionText: What does the brother eat?
questions.explanation: My brother eats a banana.

questions.questionText: What does Mother tell them to eat?
questions.explanation: Eat your apple!

passage.title: Breakfast Time

questions.explanation: We don't eat chicken for breakfast.
Điền từ trong bài đọc.

content.template: For breakfast I eat an [0] and [1].

lessons.title: Lesson 4: What's for Lunch?
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to short dialogues and identify food and drink items.

exercises.title: Learn: Listen: In the Kitchen
exercises.instructions: Listen to the conversation. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: In the Kitchen

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Yes! I want rice and chicken.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: OK. Do you want milk or water?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Water, please. I don't like milk.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: Here is an orange for you after lunch.

script.audioNotes: Clear, slow delivery with short pauses. Total duration approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: What does Linh want to eat?
questions.explanation: Linh says: I want rice and chicken.

questions.questionText: What does Linh drink?
questions.explanation: Linh says: Water, please.

choices.distractorNote: Linh says she doesn't like milk

questions.questionText: What fruit does Mum give Linh?
questions.explanation: Mum says: Here is an orange for you after lunch.

choices.distractorNote: Another fruit but not in audio

questions.questionText: Linh says 'I don't like _____.'
questions.explanation: Linh says: I don't like milk.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Yes! I want rice and chicken.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Yes. I drink milk every day.

questions.questionText: Is Hoa hungry?
questions.explanation: Hoa: Yes! I want rice and chicken.

choices.distractorNote: Cô hỏi đói — Hoa nói yes

questions.questionText: What does Hoa want to eat?
questions.explanation: I want rice and chicken.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Yes! I want rice and chicken.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Yes. I drink milk every day.

questions.questionText: What does Hoa drink every day?
questions.explanation: I drink milk every day.

questions.questionText: Who asks Are you hungry?
questions.explanation: Teacher: Are you hungry, Hoa?

questions.questionText: Match what you heard.
questions.explanation: Ghép câu hỏi và câu trả lời về bữa trưa.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Yes! I want rice and chicken.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Yes. I drink milk every day.

questions.questionText: What does Hoa drink every day?
questions.explanation: I drink milk every day.

questions.questionText: Who asks Are you hungry?
questions.explanation: Teacher: Are you hungry, Hoa?

lessons.title: Lesson 2: At the Fruit Shop
lessons.learningObjective: Identify fruit and drink orders in a market dialogue.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Three apples and two bananas, please.

questions.questionText: How many apples does Mother want?
questions.explanation: Three apples and two bananas, please.

questions.questionText: Does Mother want oranges?
questions.explanation: Seller: Do you want oranges too? Mother: Yes.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Three apples and two bananas, please.

questions.questionText: What else does Mother buy?
questions.explanation: Yes. And some water.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Three apples and two bananas, please.

questions.questionText: Who says Here you are?
questions.explanation: Seller: Here you are!
Ghép người mua và thức ăn.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Three apples and two bananas, please.

questions.questionText: Who says Here you are?
questions.explanation: Seller: Here you are!

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Breakfast Chat
lessons.learningObjective: Understand like, don't like and breakfast foods in dialogue.

questions.questionText: What doesn't Tom like?
questions.explanation: I don't like eggs.

choices.distractorNote: Bố hỏi bánh mì — Tom thích

questions.questionText: What drink does Tom want?
questions.explanation: Yes! And orange juice.

questions.questionText: What does Father ask Tom to eat too?
questions.explanation: OK. Eat your apple too.

questions.questionText: Who starts the conversation?
questions.explanation: Tom: I don't like eggs.

questions.questionText: Tom likes _____ and orange juice.
questions.explanation: Do you like bread? Yes! And orange juice.

questions.questionText: Who starts the conversation?
questions.explanation: Tom: I don't like eggs.

content.prompt: Say what you eat and drink for lunch.
content.pictureDescription: School canteen with rice, chicken, fruit and milk.
followUpQuestions: What do you eat for lunch?

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

questions.questionText: Teacher asks what you like. Best reply:
questions.explanation: Câu đầy đủ tự nhiên.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Say What You Don't Like
lessons.learningObjective: Say don't like clearly when talking about food.

questions.questionText: You don't like fish. You say:
questions.explanation: I don't like — phủ định.

questions.questionText: Father offers eggs. You don't want them. You say:
questions.explanation: I don't like eggs — từ chối lịch sự.

content.prompt: Say one food you like and one food you don't like.
content.pictureDescription: Breakfast table with egg, bread, apple and milk.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

questions.questionText: Someone offers fish. You don't like it. You say:
questions.explanation: I don't like — lịch sự.
